title: Chess Game Narration
description: Narration script for a chess explainer video. Produced by Claude from an annotation JSON; consumed by the script builder to produce the final shot list. One segment per ply gives a full guided walkthrough of the game. Optionally includes a 'challenge' block for a pause-and-think puzzle at one critical position.

title.description: Short title for the video, used on the title card and as the file name.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line subtitle for the title card (e.g., players, year, event).

segments.description: One segment per ply, in chronological order. Each segment narrates the move at that ply. For the challenge-ply (if any), this segment's text may be empty or vestigial — the challenge block supplies its visible narration instead.

plyIndex.description: Index of the ply from the annotation this segment narrates. Use -1 for position-mode segments.

text.description: Narration to be read aloud over this segment. Should align with estimatedSeconds at a natural reading pace (~150 wpm).

estimatedSeconds.description: Approximate spoken duration. Book/routine moves: 3-6s. Interesting moves: 6-10s. Critical moments: 10-18s.

depth.description: Optional commentary depth hint. 'brief' for book/forced moves, 'standard' for routine, 'deep' for inaccuracies+ and brilliancies. Used by the renderer to choose layout density.

highlightSquares.description: Optional squares to highlight on the board during this segment (e.g., ['e4', 'd5']).

showEngineLine.description: If true, the renderer should animate through the engineBest.pv for this ply after the played move.

challenge.description: Optional pause-and-think puzzle inserted in place of the normal shot at challenge.plyIndex. Composed of: a prompt asking the viewer to find the move, a silent thinking pause, 2-3 'why not X?' explanations of the wrong candidates, then a reveal of the right move. Use this only when annotation.challenge is non-null. The plyIndex must equal annotation.challenge.plyIndex.

prompt.description: Sets the puzzle: 'Pause the video. White to move. What would you play here?' plus a hint at the kind of move to look for.

thinkSeconds.description: Silent thinking pause after the prompt. Board stays on screen with no narration. Default 6.

candidates.description: Plausible-looking alternative moves with an explanation of why each one falls short. 1-3 entries. Each renders as one shot showing the candidate's arrow + the explanation narration.

san.description: SAN of the candidate move, e.g. 'Rxd7+'.

uci.description: UCI of the candidate's first move, used to draw the arrow.

text.description: Why this candidate doesn't quite work — 1-2 sentences.

reveal.description: The reveal of the right move. Shows the answer move's arrow in green. 2-4 sentences explaining what makes this move work.
